title: Review VISU_IO_DIAG Screen Readings For Operator Station Troubleshooting
summary: Use the read-only VISU_IO_DIAG screen on the Operator Station HMI to inspect alarms or informational messages, HMI temperatures, voltage levels, and displayed input states during troubleshooting.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when troubleshooting at the Operator Station and read-only diagnostic information is needed from the VISU_IO_DIAG screen, including banner messages, control temperatures, voltage levels, and station input states.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to make control changes from the VISU_IO_DIAG screen because the source states the screen is read-only.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Navigate to the VISU_IO_DIAG screen, review displayed diagnostic information, and record observations for troubleshooting.

safety_notes: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen is read-only and can be used for troubleshooting.
Do not attempt control changes from this screen.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Operator Station HMI

access_required: Access to the Operator Station HMI
system_preconditions: The Operator Station HMI is available and the VISU_IO_DIAG screen can be accessed.
external_references: Figure 4-27, Operator Station IO Diagnostics Screen

visual_references.description: Primary visual reference for the VISU_IO_DIAG screen layout, banner, temperatures, voltage levels, input sections, and screen selection drop-down.
Operator station display controls and screen navigation reference, including access to Visu_IO_Diag.

steps.title: Open the VISU_IO_DIAG screen
steps.purpose: Access the read-only diagnostics screen used for troubleshooting.
steps.instruction: Open or navigate to the VISU_IO_DIAG screen using the HMI screen selection drop-down menu.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen is displayed on the Operator Station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen opens and its diagnostic sections are visible.
steps.failure_condition: The screen cannot be accessed or the expected VISU_IO_DIAG layout is not available.

actions.label: Navigate to VISU_IO_DIAG
actions.exact_action: Use the drop-down menu to directly select the screen to view and open VISU_IO_DIAG.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_IO_DIAG layout and the screen selection drop-down.
Operator station display controls and navigation reference for accessing Visu_IO_Diag.

stop_or_escalate_if: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen cannot be accessed.
The expected diagnostic sections are not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: NOTE: This screen is read-only and can be used for troubleshooting.
The drop-down menu that can be used to directly select the screen to view.

steps.title: Check the informational banner
steps.purpose: Identify any alarms or informational messages currently displayed on the diagnostics screen.
steps.instruction: Check the informational banner for any displayed alarms or informational messages.
steps.expected_result: Any current banner message is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The banner is visible and messages can be read.
steps.failure_condition: The banner is not visible, unreadable, or does not display expected message information.

actions.label: Inspect banner messages
actions.exact_action: Review the informational banner displaying alarms or informational messages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Informational banner area on the VISU_IO_DIAG screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 1: An informational banner displaying alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Navigate through banner messages
steps.purpose: Review all available banner messages rather than only the currently displayed one.
steps.instruction: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through available banner messages and note the displayed messages.
steps.expected_result: Available banner messages are reviewed and noted.
steps.healthy_condition: The arrows allow message navigation and the displayed messages can be observed.
steps.failure_condition: Banner messages cannot be navigated or reviewed.

actions.label: Navigate banner messages
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through messages in the banner.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Banner area and the message navigation arrows on the right side.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 1: An informational banner displaying alarms or informational messages. The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.

steps.title: Review control temperatures
steps.purpose: Observe HMI internal temperature readings relevant to troubleshooting.
steps.instruction: Review the "Control Temps" section and observe the CPU and motherboard (MB) temperature readings.
steps.expected_result: CPU and motherboard temperature readings are visible and noted.
steps.healthy_condition: The Control Temps section is visible and both CPU and MB readings are displayed.
steps.failure_condition: The Control Temps section is missing or the temperature readings are not visible.

actions.label: Inspect control temperatures
actions.exact_action: Observe the CPU and motherboard (MB) temperature readings in the "Control Temps" section.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: "Control Temps" section showing CPU and MB temperature readings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 2: The "Control Temps" section provides the temperature readings of the CPU and the motherboard (MB) inside the HMI.

steps.title: Verify voltage levels
steps.purpose: Confirm the internal controller voltage reading matches the source-stated expected value.
steps.instruction: Review the "Voltage Levels" section and verify the internal controller voltage reading is 24 V.
steps.expected_result: The internal controller voltage reading is visible and compared to the expected 24 V value.
steps.healthy_condition: The Voltage Levels section shows the internal controller voltage at 24 V.
steps.failure_condition: The internal controller voltage is not 24 V or the voltage reading is not visible.

actions.label: Inspect voltage level
actions.exact_action: Review the voltage readings of the internal controller of the HMI in the "Voltage Levels" section and verify they are 24 V.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: "Voltage Levels" section showing the internal controller voltage reading.

stop_or_escalate_if: The internal controller voltage is not 24 V.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 3: The "Voltage Levels" section provides the voltage readings of the internal controller of the HMI. These should be 24 V.

steps.title: Inspect station switch input states
steps.purpose: Observe which station-mounted switch inputs are shown as on or off.
steps.instruction: Inspect the section corresponding to the switch mounted on the station and observe which inputs are shown as on and off.
steps.expected_result: The displayed on/off states for the station switch inputs are visible and noted.
steps.healthy_condition: The station switch section is visible and input states are clearly shown as on or off.
steps.failure_condition: The station switch section is missing or the input states cannot be determined.

actions.label: Inspect station switch inputs
actions.exact_action: Review the section corresponding to the switch mounted on the station to see which inputs are on and off.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Section corresponding to the switch mounted on the station and its displayed on/off input states.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 4: This section corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off.

steps.title: Open detailed input information
steps.purpose: View additional information about the displayed inputs when more detail is needed for troubleshooting.
steps.instruction: Select one of the input sections to open the window with more information about what the inputs are.
steps.expected_result: A window opens with more information about the selected inputs.
steps.healthy_condition: Selecting an input section opens a window providing more information on what the inputs are.
steps.failure_condition: Selecting an input section does not open the additional information window.

actions.label: Open input details
actions.exact_action: Select one of the input sections to open a window providing more information on what the inputs are.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Input sections on the VISU_IO_DIAG screen and the selectable area used to open more input information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 5: This section shows the inputs. Selecting one of these sections will open a window providing more information on what the inputs are.

steps.title: Record observed diagnostic information
steps.purpose: Capture the observed read-only diagnostic information for troubleshooting follow-up.
steps.instruction: Record the observed messages, temperatures, voltage reading, and input states for troubleshooting.
steps.expected_result: Observed VISU_IO_DIAG information is documented for use in troubleshooting.
steps.healthy_condition: Messages, temperatures, voltage reading, and input states have been captured.
steps.failure_condition: Required observations were not captured or key sections could not be reviewed.

actions.label: Document observations
actions.exact_action: Record the observed messages, temperatures, voltage reading, and input states for troubleshooting.

stop_or_escalate_if: Key diagnostic sections could not be reviewed.
The internal controller voltage is not 24 V.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: NOTE: This screen is read-only and can be used for troubleshooting.

Item 1: An informational banner displaying alarms or informational messages. Item 2: The "Control Temps" section provides the temperature readings of the CPU and the motherboard (MB) inside the HMI. Item 3: The "Voltage Levels" section provides the voltage readings of the internal controller of the HMI. These should be 24 V. Item 4: This section corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off. Item 5: This section shows the inputs.

success_criteria: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen is accessed successfully.
Banner messages, CPU temperature, motherboard temperature, internal controller voltage, and displayed input states are reviewed.
Any additional input detail window needed for troubleshooting is opened successfully.
Observed diagnostic information is recorded for troubleshooting.
healthy_conditions: The screen displays the informational banner, Control Temps section, Voltage Levels section, and input sections.
The internal controller voltage reading is 24 V.
Input sections can be selected to open more information about the inputs.
failure_conditions: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen cannot be accessed.
Expected diagnostic sections are not available.
The internal controller voltage is not 24 V.
Banner messages or input detail windows cannot be reviewed as described.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the screen cannot be accessed or the expected diagnostic sections are not available.
Escalate if the internal controller voltage is not 24 V.
Escalate if troubleshooting requires control changes, because the source states this screen is read-only.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall VISU_IO_DIAG screen layout, informational banner, Control Temps, Voltage Levels, station switch inputs, selectable input sections, and screen selection drop-down.
Operator station display controls and navigation reference for accessing Visu_IO_Diag.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: NOTE: This screen is read-only and can be used for troubleshooting.
Item 1: An informational banner displaying alarms or informational messages. The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.
Item 2: The "Control Temps" section provides the temperature readings of the CPU and the motherboard (MB) inside the HMI.
Item 3: The "Voltage Levels" section provides the voltage readings of the internal controller of the HMI. These should be 24 V.

Item 4: This section corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off. Item 5: This section shows the inputs. Selecting one of these sections will open a window providing more information on what the inputs are.
The drop-down menu that can be used to directly select the screen to view.

missing_details: The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before using this screen.
The source does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source does not define acceptable CPU or motherboard temperature thresholds.
The source does not specify a required recording format for captured observations.
